element_introduction_order.mastery_context: basic attack with max range of screen height; core mechanic
powerful area-of-effect weapon; stress relief mechanic designed for aggressive play in difficult situations
collectible to restore bomb stock from enemy carriers
increases helicopter firepower; collected from destroyed enemy carriers
color-coded power-up icons (red, blue, green, yellow) swapped from enemy carriers; new addition vs. Tiger-Heli
extra life pickups; bonus lives also awarded at point thresholds
must be defeated to progress; each of 10 levels ends with boss
avoidance primary mechanic; collision causes life loss and firepower reset
increased bullet density in second loop; escalation mechanic
varied enemy forces throughout; destroyed for points and power-ups

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: constant vertical scrolling with escalating difficulty; tension maintained by continuous movement until helipad reach; bomb mechanic provides aggressive relief rather than defensive safety

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: gold medal collectibles and 1-ups scattered off-path for optional scoring

difficulty_curve.early_game: Levels 1-3: introduction to standard shot, bomb mechanic, basic weapon switching; moderate enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 4-7: increasing enemy variety and firepower; denser projectile patterns; boss difficulty escalation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 8-10: high enemy density, complex bullet patterns, challenging bosses; second loop begins with significantly increased bullet density
notable_spikes: Transition to Loop 2 (difficulty spike across all stages)

notable_level_patterns.description: Vertical scrolling stage with continuous background movement; enemy waves of increasing intensity; culminates in mandatory boss fight at helipad
Each of 10 levels ends with distinct boss requiring pattern memorization and dodge timing
After defeating final boss (Level 10), game loops with same 10 levels but enemies exhibit denser bullet patterns
Enemy carriers drop colored weapon-swap icons and firepower upgrades; bomb stock managed via B-icon collection

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Twin Cobra NES article)
wikipedia (Twin Cobra - gameplay and development sections)
